results.q: Design an agent harness loop with plan→act→observe→verify. What state must persist across iterations and how do you prevent infinite loops?
results.answer_preview:
Loop: plan (JSON) → act (tools) → observe → verify → repair/stop. Persist: goal, scratchpad, tool_trace, file digests, budgets. Cap with max_iterations, no-progress stop, and nested timeouts. Emit a full trace for post-mortems.

Agent harness core loop: (1) plan with a strict JSON schema, (2) act via registered tools only, (3) observe structured tool results, (4) verify with deterministic checks (

Persist across iterations: run_id, goal, scratchpad, tool_trace[], open_todos, file digests, budget counters (iterations, tokens, wall time). Prevent infinite loops with max_iterations, n

results.q: ModelRegistry with priorities: how do you fail over from a primary API to local GGUF mid-request?
results.answer_preview:
ModelRegistry orders providers by priority. On ProviderError (quota/credit/timeout), mark primary failed for this run and continue to next. Local GGUF is last-resort always-available backend after open-model bootstrap.

results.q: Implement a safe file_write tool: atomic write, size caps, binary rejection, path jail.
results.answer_preview:
Resolve path under workspace; reject escapes. Refuse binary/huge payloads. Write to *.tmp, fsync, os.replace for atomicity. Return {ok,path,bytes}.

Tools must be schema-typed, path-jailed to workspace, size-capped, and timeout-bounded. Prefer allowlists for shell; never default-enable force-push. file_write uses temp+fsync+rename (atomic). search_code uses ripgrep with max matches and secret reda

results.q: Design multi-provider fallback chain: OpenAI→xAI→local_gguf with credit/quota detection.
results.answer_preview:
FallbackProvider walks chain; classify HTTP 401/402/429 and billing strings as failover. After APIs fail, local_runtime ensures GGUF+llama-server then answers.

Multi-provider chain: try frontier APIs, detect quota/credit/auth errors, fall back by priority to OpenRouter/local_gguf. Normalize messages to role/content. Coding mode uses low temperature and moderate max_tokens; chat can be higher. Ret

results.q: How do you normalize chat completions across OpenAI-compatible and Anthropic messages APIs?
results.answer_preview: Multi-provider chain: try frontier APIs, detect quota/credit/auth errors, fall back by priority to OpenRouter/local_gguf. Normalize messages to role/content. Coding mode uses low temperature and moderate max_tokens; chat can be higher. Retry 429/503 with exponential backoff + full jitter; do not retry 4xx auth. Validate tool names against registry before invoke. Local GGUF: health-check llama-serv

results.q: SQLite memory store schema for sessions, messages, and embeddings-optional notes.
results.answer_preview: Working memory = current context window (budgeted, pin system+goal, summarize old turns). Long-term = SQLite sessions/messages/notes. RepositoryIndex maps symbols, imports, tests, digests for planning. File digests use content hash to detect drift when files change without re-reading everything. Session isolation uses namespaces per tenant/user with wipe semantics for reset. Store tool outcome rec

results.q: Self-repair loop: run tests → parse failures → patch → retest with max iterations.
results.answer_preview:
Run pytest → parse node ids/errors → scoped patch only failing files → retest. Stop at max_iterations or when verify passes. Never delete tests to pass.

Verification must be deterministic: compile/import smoke, ruff/typecheck, unit tests, contract checks. Self-repair: parse failures → scoped patch → retest up to N times. Forbid deleting tests to greenwash. Cheap gates before expensive e2e. Prove

results.q: Mechanical acceptance checks without LLM: compile, lint, unit tests, import smoke.
results.answer_preview: Verification must be deterministic: compile/import smoke, ruff/typecheck, unit tests, contract checks. Self-repair: parse failures → scoped patch → retest up to N times. Forbid deleting tests to greenwash. Cheap gates before expensive e2e. Prove minimal diffs and scan for secrets. Quarantine flaky tests with seeds/retries rather than infinite agent loops. Use verified-answer= patterns for closed-f

results.q: Supervisor-worker multi-agent: task graph, max workers, attempt limits.
results.answer_preview: Supervisor assigns workers with attempt limits and a task graph. Partition files or use locks/merge queues to avoid clobber. Roles: planner → coder → reviewer with artifact handoffs. Message bus for kernel modules. Detect deadlock via wait-for graphs and timeouts. Budget tokens per role. Map-reduce over files then aggregate. Human gates for deploy. Correlation IDs for attribution. Debate/consensus

results.answer_preview: Coding agents: index repo → plan scoped edits → apply_patch → run tests → repair. Path jail with resolve()+relative_to(root). Unified diff apply risks fuzzy context mismatch — fail closed if hunks do not match. LRU caches need ordered dict + lock. SQLite: BEGIN IMMEDIATE for writers. results.q: Implement a thread-safe LRU cache with max size and O(1) get/put sketch.
results.answer_preview:
Thread-safe LRU: OrderedDict + Lock; on get move_to_end; on put setitem+move; while len>max: popitem(last=False). O(1) average.

results.q: Why is PEFT/LoRA preferred over full fine-tune on edge devices for continual training?
results.answer_preview:
LoRA/PEFT trains tiny A/B adapters; freezes base GGUF. Fits edge RAM/CPU; deltas federate cheaply via FedAvg without shipping full weights.

Edge continual training uses PEFT/LoRA adapters, not full fine-tunes. FedAvg merges adapter deltas weighted by samples; reject base_hash mismatches. C++ train core for hot math; Python for orchestration. Mesh discovers LAN/USB peers. Appliance boot is idempot

results.q: FedAvg over adapter deltas: weight by sample counts and detect base_hash mismatch.
results.answer_preview: Edge continual training uses PEFT/LoRA adapters, not full fine-tunes. FedAvg merges adapter deltas weighted by samples; reject base_hash mismatches. C++ train core for hot math; Python for orchestration. Mesh discovers LAN/USB peers. Appliance boot is idempotent when :8770/:8777 already listen. Network: DHCP ethernet + nmcli Wi-Fi. Q4_K_M GGUF trades quality for RAM. Hash-chain ledger for self-imp

results.q: Idempotent appliance boot when ports 8770/8777 already bound.
results.answer_preview:
On EADDRINUSE, probe /health or /mesh/hello; if healthy, mark service reused=true instead of failing boot. Idempotent appliance start.

results.q: Design install.sh that always installs the latest release/vX.Y.Z from GitHub.
results.answer_preview:
install.sh: git ls-remote release/v* and tags, sort -V, pick latest, clone --branch depth 1, verify commit, venv, pip install ., symlink current, write wrappers.

install.sh resolves latest release/vX.Y.Z (and tags), clones pinned commit, creates venv, pip installs package, wraps ~/.local/bin/sophyane. Doctor checks imports, version, provider, local model health. CI runs pytest multi-OS before tag

results.q: Python: path jail join that rejects `..` and absolute escapes under root.

root = Path(root).resolve(); target = (root / user_path).resolve(); target.relative_to(root) or raise. Reject absolute user paths that escape.
